prevalence.source: Indian Menopause Society reports; ICMR surveys
prevalence.trend: stable; earlier mean menopause age in India

burden.dalyPerCapita: quality-of-life impact from vasomotor, sleep, and urogenital symptoms

vulnerablePopulations: rural women with limited access to care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: 12 months of amenorrhea; FSH testing if diagnosis uncertain; exclude pregnancy and thyroid disease

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; opportunistic counseling in primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; limited access to menopause clinics and HRT

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care or gynecology; labs used when atypical
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle measures, calcium/vitamin D, non-hormonal therapies; menopausal hormone therapy when eligible
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low awareness, cultural stigma, and limited access to specialists
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing menopause societies and availability of low-cost generics
